titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Larry Kramer Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 8162389.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 3, 2018 at 20:17
Updated to add recent acquisitions by Rosemary K. J. Davis.
Updated to conform to baseline processing standards. Lisa Conathan, MSSU.
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: (224 boxes) + 2 roll, 4 broadside folders, 2 record album storage
did.abstract: The collection documents the career of Larry Kramer as a playwright and author, an advocate for gay rights, and an activist in the fight against AIDS. The collection consists of writings, including manuscripts and drafts of plays, books, screenplays, and articles; AIDS-related material; diaries; correspondence; photographs; printed material; audiovisual material; and other papers. There is also substantial material relating to the founding of Gay Men's Health Crisis and the AIDS Coalition to Unleash Power (ACT UP) in particular, and to the AIDS movement in general.

accessrestrict.head: Information about Access
accessrestrict.p: The bulk of the collection is open for research.
Boxes 32-36, 49-57, 182-183: Restricted material. Contact the appropriate curator for further information.
Boxes 37, 58, 178-181, 202-205, 229 (electronic materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

Boxes 29-30, 151-153, 189, 207, 228 (audiocassettes and videocassettes) and 186 (reel to reel audiotapes): Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition note
acqinfo.p: Gift of Larry Kramer, 2003-2016. November 2016 Acquisition: purchased from Larry Kramer on the Donald Windham-Sandy Campbell Collection Endowment Fund.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized into seven groupings: July 2003 Acquisition (Uncat MSS 520), July 2003 Acquisition (Uncat MSS 785), August 2007 Acquisition (Uncat MSS 975), and October 2008 Acquisition (Uncat MSS 1071), July 2011 Acquisition, February 2016 Acquisition, and November 2016 Acquisition.
Material within this collection has been organized by acquisition reflecting the fact that the collection has been acquired in increments over time.

Researchers should note that material within each acquisition overlaps with and/or relates to material found in other acquisitions. For instance, writings, correspondence, and material relating to AIDS activism can be found in most, if not all, of the groupings. In order to locate all relevant material within this collection, researchers will need to consult each acquisition described in the Collection Contents section.
Researchers should also note that similar material can be arranged differently in each acquisition, depending on how the material was organized when it was received by the library.

bioghist.head: Larry Kramer (1935- )
bioghist.p: Larry Kramer is an American playwright, author, public health advocate, and gay rights activist. He graduated from Yale University in 1957 with a B.A. in English, and is the author of several novels, including Faggots (1978), and plays, including The Normal Heart (1985) and Destiny of Me (1992), which explore themes related to gay life and the AIDS crisis. He has long been an outspoken advocate for gay rights and the fight against AIDS. He was a co-founder of the Gay Men's Health Crisis in the early 1980s, and later founded the AIDS Coalition to Unleash Power (ACT UP) in 1987. Kramer is also the author of several motion picture screenplays, including an adaptation of Women in Love , for which he was nominated for an Academy Award in 1969. He is currently working on the manuscript of an historical work called The American People .

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: Larry Kramer Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Notes
processinfo.p: Former call numbers: Uncat MSS 520, Uncat MSS 785, Uncat MSS 975, Uncat MSS 1071.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, and competing priorities. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Description of the Papers
scopecontent.p: The collection documents the career of Larry Kramer as a playwright and author, an advocate for gay rights, and an activist in the fight against AIDS. The collection consists of writings, including manuscripts and drafts of plays, books, screenplays, and articles; AIDS-related material; diaries; correspondence; photographs; printed material; audiovisual material; and other papers. There is also substantial material relating to the founding of Gay Men's Health Crisis and the AIDS Coalition to Unleash Power (ACT UP) in particular, and to the AIDS movement in general.

userestrict.head: Ownership Copyright
userestrict.p: The Larry Kramer Papers are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: This portion of the collection consists of correspondence, writings, photographs, printed material, audiotapes, videotapes, and other papers by or relating to Larry Kramer.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Organized under six headings: AIDS Activism Files; Diaries; Writings; Family Papers; Audiovisual Material; Posters.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Files contain a wide range of material documenting the AIDS crisis and relating to Kramer's activism and advocacy in the fight against AIDS, including his work with Gay Men's Health Crisis and AIDS Coalition to Unleash Power. Material includes clippings, photocopies of articles, correspondence, medical reports, publications, conference programs, press material, newsletters, notes, and writings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.p: Includes a first edition and one or more copies annotated by Kramer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: This portion of the collection consists of correspondence, bookjackets and printed material, including Lambda book report, which were transferred back to the manuscript collection from the Printed Acquisitions unit of the Beinecke Library.

did.unittitle: Keith Condon to Larry Kramer, letter
The Harvard Gay and Lesbian Review , Spring
"Larry Kramer Two Plays - The Normal Heart and The Destiny of Me", book covers

title.part: L G N Y: the Newspaper for Lesbian and Gay New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: This portion of the collection consists of correspondence, writings, Gay Men's Health Crisis files, notebooks, photographs, audio and visual recordings and other papers, which originated from Kramer's home in Connecticut.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Correspondence. II. Gay Men's Health Crisis. III. Other Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Files consist largely of incoming and outgoing correspondence and email printouts, as well as assorted other papers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Files are arranged in very rough chronological order. When material arrived, it was loose and grouped into boxes by year, beginning with the year 2000 and ending with the year 2007. The material within each box was found to span a broader range of dates, and so date ranges are supplied in the list below to better reflect the actual contents.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 49-57: Restricted material. Contact the appropriate curator for further information.
Box 58: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.physdesc: (116 boxes) + 2 broadside folders, 8 rolls, 2 record albums

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Correspondence. II. Writings. III. Writing Files. IV. Professional Papers and Research Material. V. Personal and Other Papers. VI. Audiovisual Material.
These series represent broad groupings. Material within one series overlaps with material found in other series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: This portion of the collection consists of an extensive amount of drafts and manuscripts of and files relating to Larry Kramer's writings, including plays, screenplays, and books. Also found is correspondence, printed material, notes, and other files, stemming from Kramer's film work in the 1960s and 1970s, his AIDS advocacy and activism from the late 1970s to the present day, his AIDS-related research, and other professional activities. There are also some personal and family papers. This portion of the collection originated from Kramer's apartment in New York City.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: Chronological, General, and Miscellaneous.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondence consists of incoming and outgoing letters.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: This group of correspondence represents letters that were housed together in boxes according to year. The overall grouping by year has been maintained and the groupings have been arranged in rough chronological order, however letters within each grouping are not arranged in any order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent or original folder heading, which appears in quotes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged in a very rough chronological order with original groupings left intact.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes two audiocassettes: "What's Going On?" by Paul Willcocks (circa 1985) and an unlabeled cassette (circa 1980s).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access note
accessrestrict.p: Access to original audiocassettes is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: This series contains material relating to the creation of Kramer's writings and consists primarily of numerous copies of various drafts and manuscripts of his plays, screenplays, and books. For material relating more to the production, publication, and reception of Kramer's writings, see Writings Files.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Organized into four groupings according to genre: Plays; Screenplays; Books; and Shorter Works and Other Writings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes early versions of The Destiny of Me, variously titled "Children Afraid of the Night" and "Furniture of Home".

did.unittitle: The Destiny of Me , rehearsal draft

Just Say No: A Play About a Farce

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Included are early versions of Just Say No: A Play About a Farce, titled "First Lady".

title.part: Just Say No: A Play About a Farce

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts of screenplay based on the original The Normal Heart script.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes some press clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes press material, correspondence, royalty information and press clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes early versions of "Sissies' Scrapbook," titled "Four Friends."

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Included are other versions of American Classic, titled "Sea Change".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Included are other versions of Head Games, titled "Forbidden Colors."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Included are some written material, as well as magazines used for research.

did.unittitle: "Here We Go Round the Mulberry Bush"
"Here We Go Round the Mulberry Bush"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Included are two printed items: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs: A Tale From The Brothers Grimm , translated by Randall Jarrell and Walt Disney's Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes a copy of the 1937 Robert Riskin screenplay of Lost Horizon, directed by Frank Capra.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

did.unittitle: "The Abbots" and "The Lady and the Hard Hat"
"Do Two Dead Homosexuals Equal One Dead Prostitute?"
"The Sexual Education of an American Male"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: This series consists of material relating to the production, publication, and reception of Kramer's writings (including plays, screenplays, and books). Material includes correspondence, press material, clippings, reviews, royalty statements, notes, and photographs. For material relating more to the creation of Kramer's writings, see Writings, Drafts and Manuscripts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes photographic slides.

title.part: Just Say No: A Play about a Farce
Just Say No: A Play about a Farce

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes two audiocassettes of "The Normal Heart" (circa 1993).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access note
accessrestrict.p: Access to original audiocassettes is restricted. Reference copies may be requested.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: This series consists of a wide range of material relating broadly to Kramer's various professional activities, including his film work, his AIDS advocacy and activism, especially his work with Gay Men's Health Crisis (GMHC) and AIDS Coalition to Unleash Power (ACT UP), and his non-literary writing. Material includes correspondence, printed material, notes and notebooks, research material, typescripts of articles and speeches, clippings of interviews and articles by and about Kramer, and other office files.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Organized into eight groupings: Notebooks; Film Project Files; Early Files; AIDS General; AIDS Activism; Research Material; Interviews, Articles, and Speeches; and Other Files.
Within the groupings, files are not arranged in any particular order.

did.unittitle: Beautiful and the Damned, film project
Kamp Kohut yearbooks and High School and College commencement programs
"Is Homosexuality a Form?," New School for Social Research Course

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes an award - an engraved metal tray - given to Larry Kramer by the Gay Men's Health Crisis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings, printed material, and notes pertaining to various subjects, including Roy Cohn and Proust.

did.unittitle: New York Times, correspondence and clippings
"Article, A Good News / Bad News AIDS Joke"
Interviews of and Articles by Larry Kramer
Architectural Digest, Feature on Larry Kramer
America Academy of Arts and Letters, Award
New York Public Library, correspondence regarding manuscript collection
GMHC: Interview with Larry Kramer, transcript
New York magazine , Larry Kramer interview

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes a transcript of a speech, delivered by Larry Kramer in 1994 and electronically annotated by Kramer in 2000.

did.unittitle: The Advocate, letters to editor regarding Larry Kramer article
Notebook, "First Lady, or See America First"
Lesbian and Gay Studies at Yale

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Organized into six groupings: Family Papers; Household Papers; Medical Records; Other Papers; Printed Material; and Photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes material relating to Kramer's family, including his mother Rea, his father George, and his brother Arthur.

did.unittitle: Certificates, Arthur Kramer / 1916 Class Reunion photograph, George Kramer
Diary, "My Trip to the West Coast"
Kramer, George and Rea - "Grand Circle Tour"
Photographs of Larry and Arthur Kramer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes the two parts of a tefillin, also known as phylacteries.

did.unittitle: Yale Class of 1916 - Reunion in Print

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes radiographs (x-rays).

The Night Larry Kramer Kissed Me, poster
The Tragedy of Today's Gays, poster

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes posters for movies, plays, and speaking events, as well as ones dealing with AIDS activism.

did.unittitle: Book, Singular Voices: Conversations with Americans who make a Difference , by Barbaralee Diomonstein
Snapshots and portraits of Larry Kramer, including slides
Snapshots and portraits of Larry Kramer, including slides

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access note
accessrestrict.p: Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes audio recordings of interviews, meetings, events, and a Simon & Schuster "The Normal Heart" production, as well as unlabelled cassettes with unknown material. Also includes a John Bucchino cassette (circa 1991) called "Solitude Lessons."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access note
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

Cyril Stapleton and His Orchestra performing "Women in Love," four copies

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access note
accessrestrict.p: Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes two 7 inch reel, ¼ inch-wide Kodak reel-to-reel audio tapes, which contain a stereo recording of "Women in Love."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access note
accessrestrict.p: Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes video recordings of "The Normal Heart", a four-part documentary "A Time of AIDS," Charlie Rose interviews (circa 1990s), and various other interviews and events.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1) Kramer addresses NESGLU conference 2) Documentary of NESGLU conference by Rick Shur and Adam Rosenberg 3) Newsclips, week of AZT release and Wall St. demo a) Dr. Frank Young on Larry King (CNN) b) Archie Harrison and AZT (Channel 5 WYNY) c) Cable TV censorship forbidden by Supreme Court (CNN) d) AZT and code numbers (CNN) 3) AIDS and business profits (CNN) f) AZT and code numbers (CBS) g) AIDS Defense for Kenneth Greiss, Killer (CBS) h) Wall St. demo, abortion (CBS) i) Military testing numbers (CBS) j) AIDS in Switzerland vs. Bavaria (CBS) k) Gay Olympics name battle (CNN) l) Military testing numbers (CNN) m) Koop on pregnant women (CNN) n) Wall St. demo (CNN, ABC) o) AIDS and quackery/profits (CNN) p) Reagan speech to A.M.A (CNN) q) Schlafly vs. Rep. Bruce Morrison (CNN) r) AIDS aggravated assault on army (CNN) s) Gallup poll on AUDS issues (CN)

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Correspondence. II. Writings. III. Personal and Other Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes art books and printed versions of Totanaga's artwork

did.physloc: Stored in: Oversize Box 192 and Broadside 193

arrangement.head: Arrangement Note
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: Writings, Writings by others, Correspondence, personal and other papers, and Electronic Media.

did.unittitle: "American People Volume 1" drafts and notes (16 folders)
"American People Volume 1" drafts and notes (18 folders)
"American People Volume 1" drafts and notes (4 folders)
"Just Say No" notes, reviews, and clippings
"Search For My Heart No Longer" drafts (2 folders)
"Women in Love" screenplay and drafts (6 folders)
Berman, Marjorie. "Intersection of Agitprop Theatre and AIDS Activism..." thesis
Kaufman, Moises. "Three trials of Oscar Wilde" playscript
"'Dr. Strangelove" or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb" screenplay
Engraved dish/award for Roger McFarlane from Gay Men's Health Crisis
Yale commencement programs and related papers
Eulogy and notes for Rea Kramer's funeral
Poster for Hotchkiss Gay/Straight Alliance event featuring Larry Kramer

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: Writings, Writings by others, Correspondence, personal and other papers, Diaries and notebooks, Electronic media, and Audiovisual materials.

did.unittitle: Kramer, Arthur. "Memoir of an Ancient Yalie" draft

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, event documentation, business and financial papers, theatrical programs, newsletters, press clippings, and personal papers.

did.unittitle: Architectural Digest article draft and notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts and research notes for an Architectural Digest article written by Larry Kramer.

did.unittitle: "Forum on Theatre for Public Health" program
Empty CD case for Dawn Upshaw's "I Wish It So"
"The Normal Heart" playbills and press clippings
Outer Critics Circle Award presented to Larry Kramer for "The Normal Heart" revival
Lapel pin from the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Larry Kramer personal diaries and calendars, as well as a number of notebooks used to record financial information, addresses and phone numbers.

did.unittitle: Larry Kramer's Apple computer hard drive
